metadata.title: Bioavailability and Bioequivalence Studies Submitted in NDAs or INDs — General Considerations

rules.rule_text: Applicants must use the most accurate, sensitive, and reproducible method available to demonstrate BA or BE of a product, as required under 21 CFR 320.24(a).
rules.source_section: Section III - Methods to Document BA and BE
rules.verbatim_quote: Under FDA's regulations, applicants must use the most accurate, sensitive, and reproducible method available to demonstrate BA or BE of a product (21 CFR 320.24(a)).

rules.rule_text: The reference product for BA studies must be a solution, suspension, or intravenous (IV) dosage form (21 CFR 320.25(d)(2) and (3)). Using an oral solution as reference assesses the impact of formulation on BA; using IV reference enables assessment of route of administration impact and defines absolute BA.
rules.source_section: Section II.B - Bioavailability
rules.verbatim_quote: FDA's regulations at 21 CFR 320.25 set forth guidelines for in vivo BA studies. As provided in this regulation, the reference product for BA studies should be a solution, suspension, or intravenous (IV) dosage form (21 CFR 320.25(d)(2) and (3)). The purpose of conducting a BA study with an oral solution as a reference is to assess the impact of formulation on BA. Conducting a BA study with an IV reference enables assessment of the impact of route of administration on BA and defines the absolute BA of the drug released from the drug product.

rules.rule_text: For BA and BE studies, the recommended methods in order of preference are: pharmacokinetic (PK) studies, in vitro tests predictive of human in vivo BA (IVIVC), pharmacodynamic (PD) studies, studies with clinical benefit endpoints, and other in vitro studies.
rules.source_section: Section III - Methods to Document BA and BE
rules.verbatim_quote: These include, in general order of preference, pharmacokinetic (PK) studies, in vitro tests predictive of human in vivo BA (in vitro-in vivo correlation), pharmacodynamic (PD) studies, studies with clinical benefit endpoints, and other in vitro studies.

rules.rule_text: For BA and BE studies based on PK measurements, a nonreplicate crossover study design is recommended for immediate-release and modified-release dosage forms. Sponsors have the option of using replicate designs for BE studies.
rules.source_section: Section III.A.3 - Full-Scale Study
rules.verbatim_quote: Nonreplicate crossover study designs are recommended for BA and BE studies of immediate-release and modified-release dosage forms. However, sponsors and/or applicants have the option of using replicate designs for BE studies.
rules.exceptions: Parallel design may be necessary when crossover study is problematic.
cross_references: Statistical Approaches to Establishing Bioequivalence guidance

rules.rule_text: Subjects recruited for BA or BE studies must be 18 years of age or older and capable of giving informed consent. BA and BE studies should generally be conducted in healthy volunteers.
rules.source_section: Section III.A.4 - Study Population
rules.verbatim_quote: Subjects recruited for BA or BE studies should be 18 years of age or older and capable of giving informed consent. In general, BA and BE studies should be conducted in healthy volunteers if the product can be safely administered to this population.
rules.exceptions: In some instances (e.g., when safety considerations preclude use of healthy subjects), it may be necessary to evaluate BA and BE in patients for whom the drug product is intended.

rules.rule_text: Male and female subjects must be enrolled in BA and BE studies unless there is a specific reason to exclude one sex (e.g., drug indicated for only one sex, teratogenic potential). Female subjects enrolled must not be pregnant and must not become pregnant during the study.
rules.source_section: Section III.A.4 - Study Population
rules.verbatim_quote: Male and female subjects should be enrolled in BA and BE studies unless there is a specific reason to exclude one sex. Such exclusions could be related to the drug product being indicated in only one sex or a greater potential for adverse reactions in one sex compared to the other. Female subjects enrolled in the study should not be pregnant at the beginning of the study and should not become pregnant during the study.
rules.exceptions: If a drug has the potential to be a teratogen, the drug product should be evaluated in male subjects.

rules.rule_text: Single-dose PK studies are generally recommended to assess BA and BE because they are more sensitive than steady-state studies in assessing rate and extent of release of the drug substance from the drug product into the systemic circulation.
rules.source_section: Section III.A.5 - Single-Dose and Multiple-Dose Testing
rules.verbatim_quote: This guidance generally recommends single-dose PK studies to assess BA and BE because they are generally more sensitive than steady-state studies in assessing rate and extent of release of the drug substance from the drug product into the systemic circulation.
rules.exceptions: Multiple-dose BA studies may be required when: (i) there is a difference in rate but not extent of absorption; (ii) there is excessive variability; (iii) single dose concentrations are too low for accurate determination; (iv) the product is an extended-release dosage form (21 CFR 320.27(a)(3)).

rules.rule_text: Bioanalytical methods for BA and BE studies must be accurate, precise, specific, sensitive, and reproducible.
rules.source_section: Section III.A.6 - Bioanalytical Methodology
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend that sponsors ensure that bioanalytical methods for BA and BE studies be accurate, precise, specific, sensitive, and reproducible.

rules.rule_text: BA or BE studies must be conducted under fasting conditions (after an overnight fast of at least 10 hours) except when tolerability issues are anticipated with fasting.
rules.source_section: Section III.A.7 - Administration Under Fasted/Fed Conditions
rules.verbatim_quote: The BA or BE study should be conducted under fasting conditions (after an overnight fast of at least 10 hours) except when tolerability issues are anticipated with fasting. In these cases, we recommend that applicants conduct only a fed study.
rules.exceptions: When tolerability issues are anticipated with fasting, a fed study only may be conducted.
cross_references: Food-Effect Bioavailability and Fed Bioequivalence Studies guidance

rules.rule_text: For BA studies, the active ingredient released from the dosage form, its active moiety, and when appropriate, its active metabolites must be measured in biological fluids.
rules.source_section: Section III.A.8 - Moieties to Be Measured
rules.verbatim_quote: The active ingredient that is released from the dosage form or its active moiety and, when appropriate, its active metabolites should be measured in biological fluids collected in BA studies.

rules.rule_text: For BE studies, measurement of the active ingredient or the active moiety (rather than metabolites) is generally recommended because it is more sensitive to changes in formulation performance.
rules.source_section: Section III.A.8 - Moieties to Be Measured
rules.verbatim_quote: Measurement of the active ingredient or the active moiety, rather than metabolites, is generally recommended for BE studies because the concentration-time profile of the active ingredient or the active moiety is more sensitive to changes in formulation performance than that of the metabolite, which is more reflective of metabolite formation, distribution, and elimination.
rules.exceptions: Measurement of a metabolite is necessary when active ingredient/moiety concentrations are too low to allow reliable analytical measurement, or when a metabolite is formed by presystemic metabolism and contributes meaningfully to efficacy and/or safety.

rules.rule_text: Peak exposure must be assessed by measuring Cmax obtained directly from the systemic drug concentration data without interpolation.
rules.source_section: Section III.A.9 - PK Measures of Systemic Exposure
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend that peak exposure be assessed by measuring the Cmax obtained directly from the systemic drug concentration data without interpolation.

rules.rule_text: For single-dose studies, total exposure must be measured as AUC(0-t) (from time zero to last measurable concentration) and AUC(0-inf) (from time zero to infinity). For drugs with a long half-life, truncated AUC can be used.
rules.source_section: Section III.A.9 - PK Measures of Systemic Exposure
rules.verbatim_quote: For single-dose studies, we recommend that the measurement of total exposure be: Area under the plasma, serum, or blood concentration time curve from time zero to time t (AUC(0-t)), where t is the last time point with a measurable concentration. Area under the plasma, serum, or blood concentration time curve from time zero to infinity (AUC(0-infinity)), where AUC(0-infinity) = AUC(0-t) + Ct/lambda_z.
rules.exceptions: For drugs with a long half-life, truncated AUC can be used.

rules.rule_text: For steady-state studies, total exposure must be measured as AUC(0-tau), the area under the concentration time curve from time zero to time tau (the dosing interval) at steady state.
rules.source_section: Section III.A.9 - PK Measures of Systemic Exposure
rules.verbatim_quote: For steady-state studies, we recommend that the measurement of total exposure be the area under the plasma, serum, or blood concentration time curve from time zero to time tau over a dosing interval at steady state (AUC(0-tau)), where tau is the length of the dosing interval.

rules.rule_text: The recommended method of analysis for BE comparisons is average BE using a confidence interval approach with log-transformation of exposure measures before statistical analysis.
rules.source_section: Section III.A.10 - Comparison of PK Measures in BE Studies
rules.verbatim_quote: An equivalence approach is recommended for BE comparisons. The recommended approach relies on (1) a criterion to allow the comparison, (2) a confidence interval for the criterion, and (3) a BE limit. Log-transformation of exposure measures before statistical analysis is recommended. This guidance recommends use of an average BE criterion to compare systemic exposure measures for replicate and nonreplicate BE studies of both immediate- and modified-release products.
cross_references: Statistical Approaches to Establishing Bioequivalence guidance

rules.rule_text: For oral solutions, elixirs, syrups, tinctures, or other solubilized forms, in vivo BA and/or BE are generally self-evident and a requirement for in vivo data for a product may be waived (21 CFR 320.22(b)(3)), though characterization of the pharmacokinetics of the drug is required (21 CFR 314.50(d)(3)).
rules.source_section: Section IV.A - Solutions and Other Solubilized Dosage Forms
rules.verbatim_quote: For oral solutions, elixirs, syrups, tinctures, or other solubilized forms, in vivo BA and/or BE are generally self-evident and a requirement of in vivo data for a product may be waived (21 CFR 320.22(b)(3)). In such instances, the applicant would be deemed to have complied with and fulfilled any requirement for in vivo data. Although a comparative study is not necessary, characterization of the pharmacokinetics of the drug is required (21 CFR 314.50(d)(3)).
rules.exceptions: Excipients that may alter the BA (e.g., sorbitol may reduce the BA of drugs, vitamin E may enhance the BA) in amounts sometimes used in oral liquid dosage forms may require evaluation of in vivo BA and/or BE.

rules.rule_text: For pivotal BE studies, the test batch must be representative of the production batches. The size of the test batch must be at least 10% of the planned production batch size or a minimum of 100,000 units, whichever is larger.
rules.source_section: Section IV.D - Batch Size
rules.verbatim_quote: For pivotal BE studies, the test batch should be representative of the production batches. Therefore, the size of the test batch should be at least 10% of the planned production batch size, or a minimum of 100,000 units, whichever is larger.

rules.rule_text: For immediate-release products, when using in vitro data to demonstrate BE, dissolution profiles must be generated for all strengths using an appropriate dissolution method. If dissolution characteristics are not pH- and strength-dependent, profiles in one medium are usually sufficient; otherwise, dissolution data in at least three media (pH 1.2, 4.5, and 6.8) are recommended.
rules.source_section: Section V.B.1 - Immediate-Release Formulations
rules.verbatim_quote: If an applicant seeks to demonstrate the BA or BE of immediate-release formulations for capsules, tablets, and suspensions using in vitro data, FDA recommends that sponsors generate dissolution profiles for all strengths using an appropriate dissolution method. If the dissolution results indicate that the dissolution characteristics of the product are not dependent on the pH and product strength, dissolution profiles in one medium are usually sufficient to support demonstrating BE. Otherwise, dissolution data in at least three media (e.g., pH 1.2, 4.5, and 6.8) are recommended.
cross_references: Dissolution Testing of Immediate-Release Solid Oral Dosage Forms guidance

rules.rule_text: The f2 test must be used to compare dissolution profiles from different strengths. An f2 value >= 50 indicates sufficiently similar dissolution profiles to support a biowaiver. For f2 value < 50, discussion with the appropriate review division is recommended.
rules.source_section: Section V.B.1 - Immediate-Release Formulations
rules.verbatim_quote: The f2 test should be used to compare profiles from the different strengths of the product (see FDA guidance for industry, Dissolution Testing of Immediate Release Solid Oral Dosage Forms). An f2 value >= 50 indicates a sufficiently similar dissolution profile to support a biowaiver. For an f2 value < 50, discussion with the appropriate review division is recommended to determine whether an in vivo study is needed.
rules.exceptions: The f2 approach is not suitable for rapidly dissolving drug products (e.g., >= 85% dissolved in 15 minutes or less).
cross_references: Dissolution Testing of Immediate-Release Solid Oral Dosage Forms guidance

rules.rule_text: For ER products: a single-dose food-effect study must be conducted on the highest ER strength.
rules.source_section: Section IV.C.1 - Modified-Release Products, Preapproval
rules.verbatim_quote: A single-dose food-effect study should be conducted on the highest ER strength (see the 2002 Food-Effect Guidance).
cross_references: Food-Effect Bioavailability and Fed Bioequivalence Studies guidance (2002)

rules.rule_text: For ER products with linear pharmacokinetics: a fasting study must be conducted comparing the ER product administered as a single dose at the highest strength to the IR reference administered over the least common time interval to achieve equivalent total dose.
rules.source_section: Section IV.C.1 - Modified-Release Products, Preapproval
rules.verbatim_quote: For drugs with linear pharmacokinetics over the therapeutic dose range: A fasting study should be conducted comparing the ER product administered as a single dose at the highest strength to the IR reference administered over the least common time interval to achieve equivalent total dose as for the ER product.
rules.exceptions: If for safety reasons the highest strength cannot be used, a lower strength may be acceptable.

rules.rule_text: For ER products with nonlinear pharmacokinetics: at a minimum, a single dose of the highest and lowest strengths of the ER product must be compared to their corresponding IR references administered over the ER dosing interval.
rules.source_section: Section IV.C.1 - Modified-Release Products, Preapproval
rules.verbatim_quote: For drugs with nonlinear pharmacokinetics over the therapeutic dose range: At a minimum, a single dose of the highest and lowest strengths of the ER product should be compared to their corresponding IR references administered over the ER dosing interval.
rules.exceptions: If the relative BA of intermediate ER strengths cannot be inferred based on the above studies, a single-dose fasting study for the intermediate strength(s) should also be conducted.

rules.rule_text: For ER products, a steady-state study must be conducted on the highest strength of the ER product compared to an approved IR reference dosed to achieve equivalent total dose.
rules.source_section: Section IV.C.1 - Modified-Release Products, Preapproval
rules.verbatim_quote: A steady state study should be conducted on the highest strength of the ER product compared to an approved IR reference dosed to achieve equivalent total dose as for the ER product.

rules.rule_text: The consumption of alcoholic beverages may affect MR drug product release, potentially causing dose dumping. In vitro assessments of drug release in media with various alcohol concentrations must be conducted; based on results, an in vivo BA study when administered with alcohol may be needed.
rules.source_section: Section VI.A - Alcoholic Beverage Effects on MR Drug Products
rules.verbatim_quote: The consumption of alcoholic beverages may affect the release of a drug substance from an MR formulation. The formulation may lose its MR characteristics, leading to more rapid drug release and altered systemic exposure. This more rapid drug release may have deleterious effects on the drug's safety and/or efficacy. In vitro assessments of the drug release from the drug product using media with various alcohol concentrations should be conducted. Based on the results of the in vitro assessments, an in vivo BA study of the drug product when administered with alcohol may be needed.

rules.rule_text: During development of a racemic drug product, the racemate must be measured in BA studies. Measurement of the racemate using an achiral assay is recommended for BE studies.
rules.source_section: Section VI.B - Enantiomers versus Racemates
rules.verbatim_quote: During development of a racemic drug product, the racemate should be measured in BA studies. Measurement of the racemate using an achiral assay is recommended for BE studies.
rules.exceptions: Measurement of individual enantiomers in BE studies is recommended only when all four conditions are met: (1) the enantiomers exhibit different PD characteristics; (2) the enantiomers exhibit different PK characteristics; (3) primary efficacy and safety activity resides with the minor enantiomer; and (4) nonlinear absorption is present.

rules.rule_text: For drug products with complex mixtures as active ingredients, when PK approaches are infeasible, BA and BE studies must be based on a select number of components selected based on amount of moiety in dosage form, plasma or blood levels, and biological activity.
rules.source_section: Section VI.C - Drug Products With Complex Mixtures
rules.verbatim_quote: In such cases, we recommend that BA and BE studies be based on a select number of components. Criteria for component selection typically include the amount of the moiety in the dosage form, plasma or blood levels of the moiety, and biological activity of the moiety.
rules.exceptions: When PK approaches are infeasible to assess rate and extent of absorption, PD, clinical, or in vitro approaches may be appropriate.

rules.rule_text: For long-half-life drugs (>= 24 hours), BA or BE determination may use a nonreplicate single-dose crossover study with an adequate washout period. Sample collection time must be adequate to ensure completion of gastrointestinal transit (approximately 2 to 3 days) and absorption of the drug substance.
rules.source_section: Section VI.D - Long-Half-Life Drugs
rules.verbatim_quote: In a BA or PK study involving an IR oral product with a long half-life (>= 24 hours), adequate characterization of the half-life should include blood sampling over a long period of time. For BA or BE determination of a drug product containing a drug with a long half-life, a nonreplicate, single-dose, crossover study can be conducted, provided an adequate washout period is used... we recommend that the sample collection time be adequate to ensure completion of gastrointestinal transit (approximately 2 to 3 days) of the drug product and absorption of the drug substance.
rules.exceptions: If the crossover study is problematic, a study with a parallel design can be used.

rules.rule_text: For drug products intended for local GI action, documentation of BA and BE can be achieved through pharmacokinetics, an acceptable PD endpoint, clinical efficacy and safety studies, and/or suitably designed and validated in vitro studies.
rules.source_section: Section VI.E - Orally Administered Drugs Intended for Local Action
rules.verbatim_quote: Documentation of BA and BE when the drug substance produces its effects by local action in the gastrointestinal tract can be achieved either by using pharmacokinetics, an acceptable PD end point, clinical efficacy and safety studies, and/or suitably designed and validated in vitro studies, as appropriate.

rules.rule_text: For combination drug products, the BA study must compare rate and extent of absorption of each active drug ingredient or therapeutic moiety in the combination drug product to the rate and extent of absorption of each active drug ingredient administered concurrently in separate single-ingredient preparations (21 CFR 320.25(g)).
rules.source_section: Section VI.F - Combination/Coadministered Drug Products
rules.verbatim_quote: Generally, the purpose of an in vivo BA study involving a combination drug product is to compare the rate and extent of absorption of each active drug ingredient or therapeutic moiety in the combination drug product to the rate and extent of absorption of each active drug ingredient or therapeutic moiety administered concurrently in separate single-ingredient preparations (21 CFR 320.25(g)).

rules.rule_text: For combination drug products, the following studies must be conducted: (1) a two-treatment, single-dose, fasting study of the combination product versus single-ingredient products administered concurrently; and (2) a single-dose food-effect study on the combination drug product.
rules.source_section: Section VI.F - Combination/Coadministered Drug Products
rules.verbatim_quote: For the purpose of defining BA or determining BE when required, this guidance recommends that the following studies be conducted for a combination drug product: A two-treatment, single-dose, fasting study of the combination drug product versus single-ingredient drug products administered concurrently as a single treatment or an approved combination product containing the same active ingredients. This study should use the highest strength of the combination product with matching doses of individual drug products. A single-dose, food-effect study on the combination drug product.
rules.exceptions: Certain alternative study designs may be acceptable depending on the specific situation.

rules.rule_text: For endogenous substances, baseline concentrations of the endogenous compound in blood (plasma) must be measured and subtracted from total concentrations measured after drug administration to estimate actual drug availability.
rules.source_section: Section VI.G - Endogenous Substances
rules.verbatim_quote: In most cases, it is important to measure and approximate the baseline endogenous levels of the compound in blood (plasma) and subtract these levels from the total concentrations measured from each subject after the drug product is administered. In this way, an estimate of actual drug availability from the drug product can be achieved, and therefore BA and BE can be assessed.
rules.exceptions: An alternative approach might be to enroll patients with low or no production of the endogenous substance instead of healthy volunteers.

rules.rule_text: For endogenous substance studies, baseline concentrations must be determined for each dosing period with period-specific baseline corrections. If a negative plasma concentration value results after baseline correction, this should be set to 0 prior to calculating baseline-corrected AUC.
rules.source_section: Section VI.G - Endogenous Substances
rules.verbatim_quote: In either case, baseline concentrations should be determined for each dosing period, and baseline corrections should be period-specific. If a negative plasma concentration value results after baseline correction, this should be set to 0 prior to calculating the baseline-corrected AUC.

rules.rule_text: For highly variable drugs with intrasubject variability >= 30%, a reference-scaled BE approach using a replicate design can be considered. This approach should impose a limit of 0.8 to 1.25 on the geometric mean ratio.
rules.source_section: Section VI.H - Drug Products With High Intrasubject Variability
rules.verbatim_quote: In addition to the traditional approach and the use of average BE using replicate designs, the use of a reference-scaled BE approach using a replicate design can be considered. This approach should be reserved for drugs that demonstrate a high intrasubject variability (>=30%). The reference-scaled average BE approach adjusts the BE limits of highly variable drugs by scaling to the within-subject variability of the reference product in the study and imposes a limit of 0.8 to 1.25 on the geometric mean ratio.
rules.exceptions: The appropriate review division should be consulted when planning the use of the reference-scaled BE approach.
cross_references: Statistical Approaches to Establishing Bioequivalence guidance

rules.rule_text: BA and BE studies must be conducted under fasting conditions with the study drug administered with about 8 ounces (240 milliliters) of water. Subjects must abstain from alcohol for 24 hours before each study period and until after the last sample from each period is collected.
rules.source_section: Appendix A - General Study Design and Data Handling
rules.verbatim_quote: The BA or BE study should be conducted under fasting conditions (after an overnight fast of at least 10 hours)... The test and reference products should be administered with about 8 ounces (240 milliliters) of water to an appropriate number of subjects... Before and during each study phase, we recommend that subjects (1) be allowed water as desired except for 1 hour before and after drug administration, (2) be provided standard meals no less than 4 hours after drug administration, and (3) abstain from alcohol for 24 hours before each study period and until after the last sample from each period is collected.

rules.rule_text: An adequate washout period (e.g., >= 5 half-lives of the moieties to be measured) must separate each treatment in a BA or BE study.
rules.source_section: Appendix A - General Study Design and Data Handling
rules.verbatim_quote: An adequate washout period (e.g., >=5 half-lives of the moieties to be measured) should separate each treatment.

rules.rule_text: The assayed drug content of the test product must not differ from the reference product by more than +/- 5 percent. The sponsor must include a statement of the composition of the test product and, if possible, a side-by-side comparison of test and reference listed product compositions.
rules.source_section: Appendix A - General Study Design and Data Handling
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend that the assayed drug content of the test product batch not differ from the reference product by more than +/- 5 percent. The sponsor should include a statement of the composition of the test product and, if possible, a side-by-side comparison of the compositions of test and reference listed products.

rules.rule_text: Samples of the test and reference listed product must be retained for at least 5 years, in accordance with 21 CFR 320.38 and 21 CFR 320.63.
rules.source_section: Appendix A - General Study Design and Data Handling
rules.verbatim_quote: In accordance with 21 CFR 320.38, and 21 CFR 320.63, samples of the test and reference listed product must be retained for at least 5 years.

rules.rule_text: For most BA/BE studies, 12 to 18 samples including a pre-dose sample must be collected per subject per dose. Sampling must continue for at least three or more terminal elimination half-lives of the drug to capture 90 percent of the relevant AUC.
rules.source_section: Appendix A - Sample Collection and Sampling Times
rules.verbatim_quote: For most drugs we recommend that 12 to 18 samples, including a pre-dose sample, be collected per subject per dose. This sampling should continue for at least three or more terminal elimination half-lives of the drug to capture 90 percent of the relevant AUC.

rules.rule_text: If the pre-dose concentration is > 5 percent of Cmax value in a subject, that subject must be dropped from all PK evaluations, though the subject's data should be reported and the subject should be included in safety evaluations.
rules.source_section: Appendix A - Subjects with Pre-Dose Plasma Concentrations
rules.verbatim_quote: If the pre-dose concentration is <= 5 percent of Cmax value in that subject, the subject's data can be included in all PK measurements and calculations. We recommend that if the pre-dose value is > 5 percent of Cmax, the subject should be dropped from all PK evaluations. The subject data should be reported and the subject should be included in safety evaluations.

rules.rule_text: For BE data submission, applicants must not round off CI values; to pass a CI limit of 80 to 125 percent, the value should be at least 80.00 percent and not more than 125.00 percent.
rules.source_section: Appendix A - Rounding off of Confidence Interval Values
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend that applicants not round off CI values; therefore, to pass a CI limit of 80 to 125 percent, the value should be at least 80.00 percent and not more than 125.00 percent.
cross_references: Statistical Approaches to Establishing Bioequivalence guidance
